rationale: Title X is cleaner and more publishable for PACE. It states the clinical sequence, procedure, complication profile, and article type without overloading the reader. Title Y adds useful specifics about individualized antithrombotic management and patient age, but it becomes too long and reads more like a subtitle-heavy case report title, reducing clarity and editorial polish.
loser_weakness: Title Y is more informative, but its length and dense phrasing weaken readability and appeal.
